threatgraph_indicators.description: A process loaded a module that shares characteristics with a known malicious file. Review the modules loaded by the process.
A file written to the file-system was classified as Adware/PUP based on its SHA256 hash.

A process attempted to establish an unusual connection over a commonly used port, possibly to a command and control server. Adversaries can use this to bypass firewalls and evade detection. Review the process tree.

description: A process appears to be using a Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) application in a suspicious manner. This may be an attempt to gather information, gain access, or drop other malicious payloads. Investigate the process tree and the detected process.
name: SuspiciousRMMUsage

description: LSASS process accessed from a signed executable with untrusted root authority
name: RemoteOpenLsassUntrusted

description: An executable with two file name extensions was written. Adversaries can masquerade malware with a seemingly safe file extension to evade detection. Review the executable.
name: DoubleExtensionWritten

threatgraph_indicators.description: An executable with two file name extensions was written. Adversaries can masquerade malware with a seemingly safe file extension to evade detection. Review the executable.
LSASS process accessed from a signed executable with untrusted root authority

A process appears to be using a Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) application in a suspicious manner. This may be an attempt to gather information, gain access, or drop other malicious payloads. Investigate the process tree and the detected process.
A process appears to be suspiciously executing code via Powershell. Investigate the command line and process tree.
A process appears to have performed a system call by bypassing UMPPC hooks.

description: A process appears to be suspiciously executing code via Powershell. Investigate the command line and process tree.
name: GenericPowershellExecution

description: A file written to the file-system was classified as Adware/PUP based on its SHA256 hash.
name: OnWrite-HashPupAdwareCrowdStrikeSha256
pattern_disposition_description: Detection/Quarantine, standard detection and quarantine was attempted.
priority_explanation: [MOD] The disposition for the detection: quarantined file

description: A process attempted to establish an unusual connection over a commonly used port, possibly to a command and control server. Adversaries can use this to bypass firewalls and evade detection. Review the process tree.
name: GenericCommonlyUsedPortC2

description: A process loaded a module that shares characteristics with a known malicious file. Review the modules loaded by the process.
name: MaliciousModule
